description: Create 8 security vulnerability triage tasks in new ccb_security suite. Agent receives CVE advisory text and must locate vulnerable code, trace attack paths, and assess exploitability. Analysis only — no code fixes. Uses weighted checklist scoring.

userStories.title: Create sec-cve-001: Locate vulnerable code from curl CVE
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task where the agent receives a curl CVE advisory (with public patch) and must locate the vulnerable function in the curl/curl codebase. Choose a well-documented CVE (e.g., CVE-2023-38545 SOCKS5 heap buffer overflow). Pin the Dockerfile to the pre-fix commit. Agent must find the vulnerable file and function WITHOUT being told where to look.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-cve-001/ has complete task files (task.toml, instruction.md, environment/Dockerfile, tests/test.sh, tests/ground_truth.json)
task.toml has category=cve_triage, language=c, difficulty=hard, time_limit_sec=1200
instruction.md provides CVE advisory text (description, severity, affected versions) but NOT the vulnerable file or function
Dockerfile clones curl/curl at a pre-fix commit SHA (vulnerable code present)
ground_truth.json required_findings include: vulnerable function name, vulnerable file path, vulnerability type (e.g., buffer overflow)
ground_truth.json file_references include the specific source file(s)
ground_truth.json negative_checks: must NOT blame wrong module (e.g., must not blame TLS layer if vuln is in SOCKS5)
Output format: /logs/agent/triage.md
Task registered in configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json and security_2config.sh
userStories.notes: CVE-2023-38545: heap overflow in SOCKS5 proxy handshake. Fix commit: fb4415d8aee6c1045be932a34fe6107c2f5ed147. Pre-fix: 09e25b9d94f4106eac8ca3a43b221bdc66f405e4. Vulnerable file: lib/socks.c, function: do_SOCKS5(). Use web search to find CVE details and fix commit.

userStories.title: Create sec-cve-002: Locate vulnerable code from Envoy CVE
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task using a real Envoy security advisory. Choose a CVE with a public patch (Envoy publishes detailed security advisories). Agent must locate vulnerable code in envoyproxy/envoy.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-cve-002/ has complete task files
Uses a real Envoy CVE with public patch
Dockerfile pins to pre-fix commit
ground_truth.json has 5+ required_findings, 3+ file_references
Task registered
userStories.notes: CVE-2024-45810: use-after-free in HTTP async client sendLocalReply(). Vulnerable version: v1.31.1 (fixed in 1.31.2). Root cause: router destroyed at async stream destructor while stream undergoes deferred deletion, causing segfault when onDestroy() references destroyed decoder. Trigger: ext_authz + request mirroring + WebSocket upgrade. Vulnerable files: source/common/http/async_client_impl.cc, router components. Advisory: GHSA-qm74-x36m-555q.

userStories.title: Create sec-cve-003: Locate vulnerable code from Go stdlib or Terraform CVE
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a third CVE location task using either a Go standard library vulnerability or a Terraform CVE, for language diversity.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-cve-003/ has complete task files
Uses a real CVE in a Go codebase with public patch
ground_truth.json has 5+ findings
Task registered
userStories.notes: CVE-2023-39325: HTTP/2 rapid reset DoS in golang.org/x/net. Fix commit: b225e7ca6dde1ef5a5ae5ce922861bda011cfabd. Pre-fix: 88194ad8ab44a02ea952c169883c3f57db6cf9f4. Vulnerable files: http2/server.go (processHeaders, runHandler functions). Attack: rapid RST_STREAM frames causing unbounded goroutine spawning.

userStories.title: Create sec-reach-001: Trace attack path reachability in curl
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task where the agent is given a known vulnerable function in curl and must trace whether it's reachable from external HTTP input. Agent must produce the full call chain from HTTP handler to vulnerable function.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-reach-001/ has complete task files
instruction.md provides the vulnerable function name and asks: 'Is this function reachable from external HTTP input? Trace the complete call path.'
ground_truth.json causal_chain includes the complete call path (4+ functions)
ground_truth.json required_findings includes exploitability assessment (reachable vs mitigated)
Task uses the same or different curl CVE as sec-cve-001, but provides the function name upfront
Task registered
userStories.notes: Task completed. Uses same curl SOCKS5 CVE as sec-cve-001 but provides do_SOCKS5() function name upfront. Ground truth includes 4-step call chain (Curl_connect → Curl_connecthost → socks_proxy_connect → do_SOCKS5). Reachability assessment: YES, exploitable via --proxy socks5://...

userStories.title: Create sec-reach-002: Trace attack path in Envoy with UNREACHABLE vulnerability
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a reachability task where the vulnerable function is NOT actually reachable from external input (mitigated by an intermediate check). Agent must correctly identify this as NOT exploitable.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-reach-002/ has complete task files
The vulnerable function exists but is guarded by a check that prevents exploitation
ground_truth.json required_findings includes: assessment=mitigated/unreachable
ground_truth.json causal_chain shows the path from entry point to the guard that blocks exploitation
ground_truth.json negative_checks: must NOT claim the vulnerability is exploitable
Task registered
userStories.notes: Critical for calibration: at least 1 task must have answer=not-exploitable. Choose a function that LOOKS dangerous but has upstream validation. Envoy's input validation is extensive — find a case where a theoretically vulnerable parser is behind a length check.

userStories.title: Create sec-reach-003: Trace attack path in Kafka (Java)
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a reachability task in a Java codebase for language diversity. Use apache/kafka.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-reach-003/ has complete task files
Uses apache/kafka codebase, language=java
ground_truth.json has complete call path with 4+ functions
Task registered
userStories.notes: Kafka has had several deserialization and authentication-bypass CVEs. Choose one with clear call path from network handler to vulnerable code.

userStories.title: Create sec-transitive-001: Transitive dependency vulnerability (affected)
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task where a vulnerability in a transitive dependency IS reachable by the consuming project. Agent must trace the import chain and determine the project IS affected.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-transitive-001/ has complete task files
Dockerfile clones 2 repos: the vulnerable dependency and the consuming project
instruction.md describes the vulnerability in dependency X and asks if project Y is affected
ground_truth.json includes: import chain, specific vulnerable function called by Y, assessment=affected
Task registered
userStories.notes: Good candidates: a Go project using a vulnerable version of golang.org/x/net or golang.org/x/crypto. The consuming project must actually call the vulnerable function path.

userStories.title: Create sec-transitive-002: Transitive dependency vulnerability (NOT affected)
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task where a vulnerability exists in a dependency but the consuming project does NOT use the vulnerable code path. Agent must correctly determine NOT affected.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_security/sec-transitive-002/ has complete task files
The consuming project imports the vulnerable package but never calls the vulnerable function
ground_truth.json required_findings: assessment=not-affected, unused_function identified
ground_truth.json negative_checks: must NOT claim the project is affected
Task registered
userStories.notes: Pair with sec-transitive-001 for calibration. One affected, one not. The 'not affected' case is harder — agent must prove a negative by exhaustively checking all call paths.
